title: 3.1.2 Inorganic Ions
description: Roles of inorganic ions in living organisms.

questions.question: What are inorganic ions?
options: Charged entities because they are atoms with unshared electrons in their outer shell
Neutral atoms with full outer shells
questions.correctAnswer: Charged entities because they are atoms with unshared electrons in their outer shell

questions.question: What are the two forms in which inorganic ions are found in living bodies?
options: Free and dissolved in the cytoplasm and associated with complex organic substances
questions.correctAnswer: Free and dissolved in the cytoplasm and associated with complex organic substances

questions.question: What happens if there are variations in inorganic ion concentrations inside the human body?
What are inorganic ions frequently referred to as in the context of the human body?
How are inorganic ions classified based on daily dietary requirement?
Which ions are considered the most important for maintaining balance and equilibrium in living systems?
Where are hydrogen ions released in the cells?
In which body fluids are hydrogen ions found?
options: Cytoplasm, inter-cellular fluid, blood, cerebrospinal fluid, and other body fluids
questions.correctAnswer: Cytoplasm, inter-cellular fluid, blood, cerebrospinal fluid, and other body fluids

questions.question: How is the concentration of hydrogen ions expressed?
What is the range of the $pH$ scale?
What is the relationship between $pH$ value and hydrogen ion concentration?
options: The higher the $pH$, the lower the hydrogen ion concentration
$pH$ and hydrogen ion concentration are identical
The higher the $pH$, the higher the hydrogen ion concentration
questions.correctAnswer: The higher the $pH$, the lower the hydrogen ion concentration

questions.question: What is the ranking of sodium ions among positively charged ions in the body?
Where is the concentration of sodium ions higher?
What is the average daily salt need for a person?
How does the body eliminate excess salt?
What dietary recommendation applies to patients with high blood pressure regarding salt?
Sodium is a cation that is common in body fluids but present in lower concentrations where?
What type of ions are potassium ions?
How do potassium ions compare to sodium ions in terms of cellular location?
options: Potassium is only found in blood
Potassium is only found in bones
Potassium is the major intracellular cation
questions.correctAnswer: Potassium is the major intracellular cation

questions.question: What is the daily potassium requirement for an average human being?
Which foods are abundant sources of potassium?
options: Bananas, oranges, potatoes, chicken, and liver
questions.correctAnswer: Bananas, oranges, potatoes, chicken, and liver

questions.question: What happens to potassium concentration in plasma during hemolysis of red blood cells?
Which ion is the most abundant inorganic ion present in the human body?
Where is calcium mainly present in the body?
What is the daily calcium requirement for a healthy human?
Which foods are ideal sources of calcium?
How much of our body weight does calcium make up?
What percentage of calcium is present in bones and teeth?
What is the calcium concentration in plasma?
What type of charge do phosphate ions carry?
How much phosphate does the body of an average man contain?
Where are most phosphate ions found in the body?
options: Combined with calcium in bones and teeth
questions.correctAnswer: Combined with calcium in bones and teeth

questions.question: What is the daily phosphate requirement for an adult male?
What is the plasma concentration of phosphate ions?
What type of charge do chloride ions carry?
Where are chloride ions primarily present?
What is the average daily requirement of chloride ions?
In what form are chloride ions usually taken in the diet?
options: As NaCl present in cooked food
questions.correctAnswer: As NaCl present in cooked food

questions.question: What is the role of chloride ions in the stomach?
options: They are required to make hydrochloric acid (HCl) present in gastric juice
questions.correctAnswer: They are required to make hydrochloric acid (HCl) present in gastric juice

questions.question: What enzyme is activated by chloride ions?
What type of charge do iron ions carry?
What percentage of iron ions in the body is present in haemoglobin within red blood cells?
Which foods are sources of iron?
options: Legumes, red meat, liver, spinach, pumpkin seeds, fish
questions.correctAnswer: Legumes, red meat, liver, spinach, pumpkin seeds, fish

questions.question: What is the role of iron as a component of haemoglobin and myoglobin?
options: Transport of oxygen and carbon dioxide
questions.correctAnswer: Transport of oxygen and carbon dioxide

questions.question: What is the role of iron in the electron transport chain?
options: It is an essential component of cytochromes
questions.correctAnswer: It is an essential component of cytochromes

questions.question: What is iron-deficiency anemia?
options: A common condition in individuals with an iron-deficient diet
Abnormal iron deposits in the liver
questions.correctAnswer: A common condition in individuals with an iron-deficient diet

questions.question: What is hemosiderosis?
options: Characterized by excess iron in the body
questions.correctAnswer: Characterized by excess iron in the body

questions.question: What is hemochromatosis?
options: A condition where abnormal iron deposits form in the liver, spleen, skin, and pancreas
questions.correctAnswer: A condition where abnormal iron deposits form in the liver, spleen, skin, and pancreas

questions.question: What type of charge do copper ions carry?
Which foods are sources of copper?
options: Shellfish, seeds and nuts, organ meats, wheat, whole-grain, chocolate
questions.correctAnswer: Shellfish, seeds and nuts, organ meats, wheat, whole-grain, chocolate

questions.question: What is copper needed for in the body?
options: Synthesis of haemoglobin, collagen, and elastin
questions.correctAnswer: Synthesis of haemoglobin, collagen, and elastin

questions.question: What is Wilson disease?
options: A rare condition caused by abnormal copper metabolism
A condition caused by abnormal iron metabolism
questions.correctAnswer: A rare condition caused by abnormal copper metabolism

questions.question: What are the effects of Wilson disease on the liver and brain?
options: Abnormal copper deposition causing hepatic cirrhosis and brain damage
questions.correctAnswer: Abnormal copper deposition causing hepatic cirrhosis and brain damage

questions.question: What can copper deposition in the kidneys cause?
Where is iodine naturally found?
options: In the earth's soil and ocean waters
questions.correctAnswer: In the earth's soil and ocean waters

questions.question: In what form is iodine most widely available in the diet?
Which gland takes up iodine to produce hormones?
Where is the thyroid gland located?
options: At the base of the front of the neck
questions.correctAnswer: At the base of the front of the neck

questions.question: What do thyroid hormones control?
What happens when iodine is lacking in the diet?
What condition results from a low or underactive thyroid gland?
What is goiter?
What is hyperthyroidism?
What is the daily iodine requirement for adolescents and adults?
Which vegetable is a source of iron?
What is the role of hydrochloric acid (HCl) in the stomach besides digesting proteins?
options: It kills bacteria and other pathogens in food
questions.correctAnswer: It kills bacteria and other pathogens in food

questions.question: Where is the 1% of calcium found outside bones and teeth located?
options: In the cytoplasm of other cells and extracellular fluid
questions.correctAnswer: In the cytoplasm of other cells and extracellular fluid

questions.question: What are chloride ions involved in maintaining?
options: Osmotic pressure, fluid balance, and acid-base balance
questions.correctAnswer: Osmotic pressure, fluid balance, and acid-base balance

questions.question: What is the role of iron in white blood cells?
options: It is a component of the peroxidase enzyme, a lysosomal enzyme for killing bacteria
It is a component of the antibody
questions.correctAnswer: It is a component of the peroxidase enzyme, a lysosomal enzyme for killing bacteria
